Chapter 1: The Two Alchemists.action: The first chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Two Alchemists"
a young boy suffers the aftermath of a mysterious and forbidden alchemical ritual gone wrong in the cellar of a house
the boy bleeds profusely from the mangled stump of his left leg
the boy cries out for his brother Alphonse
the boy receives no response; his brother is gone
The boy and a large man clad in steel plate armor arrive in the frontier town of Reole
Every radio broadcasts the religious teachings and praises of the sun god Leto as proclaimed by Father Cornello
A small incident forces the two strangers to reveal that they are alchemists
The two strangers introduce themselves as the Elric brothers: Alphonse (in armor) and Edward (the smaller, State Alchemist known as the "Fullmetal Alchemist")
The Elric brothers meet Rose Thomas
Rose becomes Cornello's most devout adherent after being told her deceased lover could be resurrected through Leto's power
Edward becomes suspicious of Cornello's claims and decides to investigate further
The Elrics encounter Rose in the church
Edward confronts Rose’s blind religious faith with scientific facts to disprove the existence of gods, miracles, and resurrections
Edward and Alphonse witness a public display of Cornello’s “miraculous power”
Edward and Alphonse begin to suspect an unnatural force behind Cornello’s power
Edward and Alphonse request an audience with Cornello
Cornello fears the State Military has sent the Elrics to investigate his secret plans
Cornello plans to have the Elrics quietly murdered
Brother Cray attempts to carry out Cornello’s order to murder the Elrics
Brother Cray and his men are subdued by Edward and Alphonse
The struggle knocks off Alphonse’s helmet, revealing the armor suit to be empty
Rose is horrified by the revelation about Alphonse
Edward and Alphonse explain to Rose that Al’s state is the result of meddling in “God’s domain”
The Elrics challenge Rose to confront the truth about her leader
Edward and Alphonse accuse Cornello of using alchemy to fool his congregation
Edward and Alphonse claim Cornello is using the Philosopher’s Stone for transmutations
Cornello admits his deception and possession of the Philosopher’s Stone
Edward demands Cornello hand over the Philosopher’s Stone
Cornello refuses to hand over the Stone, citing how easily he fooled the town
Rose is swayed by Cornello’s renewed promise to resurrect her sweetheart
Rose stands idly by while Cornello attempts to kill the Elrics by unleashing a chimera
Cornello unleashes a chimera to attack the Elrics
Edward takes on the chimera himself
Edward performs alchemy without a Transmutation Circle
Edward’s alchemy fails to defeat the chimera
Edward reveals his right arm and left leg are automail prostheses
Edward threatens to show Cornello the difference between them

Chapter 2: The Price of Life.action: The 2nd chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Price of Life (命の代価, Inochi no Daika?)"
Cornello chastises the boys for committing alchemy's greatest taboo, Human Transmutation
Alphonse explains to Rose that he and Edward performed Human Transmutation years ago to resurrect their late mother
The ritual fails, causing Ed to lose his left leg and Alphonse to lose his entire body
Alphonse loses consciousness after the failed ritual
Alphonse awakens as a soul inside a suit of armor, the result of Edward's rushed transmutation
Edward sacrifices his right arm to perform the transmutation that binds Alphonse's soul to the armor
Edward informs Rose that their current state is the result of trifling with the powers of life and death
Edward warns Rose not to become involved with such things
Father Cornello attacks with the power of the Philosopher's Stone
Edward and Alphonse escape the hall with Rose, defeating many of Cornello's guards
Edward and Alphonse escape the hall with Rose, defeating many of Cornello's guards
Edward and Alphonse escape the hall with Rose, defeating many of Cornello's guards
Edward comes across Cornello's broadcast room and devises a fiendish plan
Alphonse heads outside with Rose to transmute a makeshift loudspeaker
Rose asks if the sacrifices they made resulted in the resurrection of their mother
Alphonse responds that the creature born from the ritual hadn't even been human
Alphonse explains they have given up on resurrecting their mother and now seek to restore each other's bodies
Cornello comes across Edward seated casually in his broadcast office
Edward advises Cornello to give up, saying his plans will be spread to the town through Rose
Cornello resists Edward's advice
Cornello rants madly about brainwashing the people of Reole into believing his phony words and false miracles
Cornello claims he will mold the people into faithful adherents and use them to conquer the entire country
Ed reveals that the broadcast microphone has been on since the beginning of the rant
Alphonse transmits their conversation to the entire town over the loudspeaker
Edward begins his counterattack, transmuting the outer plating of his automail arm into a blade to destroy the prophet's weapon
Cornello attempts to fight back
Cornello's arm is mangled in an alchemical rebound
The Stone on Cornello's ring crumbles to dust
Edward understands that the prophet's stone was a fake
Edward transmutes a giant statue of the god Leto to knock Cornello unconscious
Edward informs Alphonse of the false Stone's destruction
Edward laments that they must continue their search somewhere else
Rose demands to know what she is supposed to believe in now that the Elrics have destroyed her last chance for happiness
Edward tells Rose she must stand up and walk forward of her own power, grateful that she has two strong legs
The people of Reole riot around the church
Cornello marches back to his secluded hall, intent on saving face and taking revenge
Cornello is met by Lust and Gluttony
Lust and Gluttony scold Cornello for letting his plan fall apart
Lust and Gluttony reveal they were only using Cornello and gave him a Philosopher's Stone to stir up trouble
Cornello, furious, charges at Lust
Lust kills Cornello instantly by extending her razor-sharp fingernails and impaling him through the skull
Gluttony eats Cornello's body
Lust laments that their Father will not be pleased

Chapter 3: The Mining Town.action: The 3rd chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Mining Town"
Edward and Alphonse arrive in the frontier town of Youswell to inspect the coal mines
Edward and Alphonse meet a young boy named Kyle, who refers them to an inn owned by his father Halling
Edward and Alphonse learn it will cost two hundred thousand cenz to stay the night due to the village's low income
Edward and Alphonse discount the price by performing free alchemy for the grateful townspeople
The townspeople become astounded and grateful for the alchemy
The townspeople turn against Edward when they learn he is a State Alchemist and kick him out for being a "dog of the military"
Alphonse is allowed to stay behind due to his civilian status
Alphonse questions Kyle and the others about the town's distaste for the military
Alphonse discovers that Lt. Yoki owns the land and exploits the miners by capping wages and raising taxes, bribing his superiors
Halling notes his distaste for State Alchemists is due to their rejection of the alchemist code
Alphonse brings Edward food outside and relates all of this to him
Lt. Yoki arrives at the inn with two soldiers, demanding back taxes
Kyle insults Lt. Yoki
Lt. Yoki orders a soldier to discipline Kyle
Edward intercedes and protects Kyle with his automail arm
Edward introduces himself to Yoki as a State Alchemist
Yoki sycophantically escorts Edward to his own mansion for the night
Yoki bribes Edward to grease the wheels of his own promotion and raise his stock in Central City
Edward overhears Yoki ordering his men to burn down Halling's inn
Yoki's men burn down Halling's inn
Edward investigates in the morning and finds the inn has been destroyed
Edward suggests the townspeople move somewhere else
Halling explains that the townspeople's personal attachments prevent them from leaving
Edward decides to help
Edward secretly transmutes mining waste into gold bars
Edward approaches Yoki and asks to buy the deed to the town and mines
Edward advises Yoki to document the purchase as a gift to keep it off the government's radar
Edward returns to the wreckage of Halling's inn with the deed and exchanges it to the foreman for a night's stay
Edward proves he is still in service of the people despite being a State Alchemist
Yoki arrives, furious that the gold bars have turned to dust and pebbles
Edward had transmuted the gold back before leaving the mansion
Yoki demands the deed back, claiming it was bought fraudulently
Edward reminds Yoki that the deed was given as a gift, leaving Yoki powerless
The coal miners vent their frustrations on Yoki and his men
Edward declares he will report Yoki's corrupt actions to Central Command

Chapter 4: Battle on the Train.action: Battle on the Train is introduced as the 4th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga
Major General Hakuro enjoys a leisurely train ride from New Optain to East City with his wife and two small children
The train is hijacked by several members of the eastern extremist group "Blue Squad" led by Bald
The Colonel in East City receives a manifesto of Bald's demands
The Colonel learns that Edward Elric is listed among the passengers on the hijacked train
Edward wakes from his nap on the locomotive
Edward, irritated by the hijackers' rudeness, quickly subdues the two armed hijackers stationed in his car
Alphonse is chagrined by Edward's actions
fellow passengers are wary
The Elrics take stock of the situation and plan to take out the other terrorists
Edward heads up to the engine room via the train's roof
Alphonse follows through the train cars, taking out hijackers as he goes
Bald receives word from his men that the henchmen in the rear cars have stopped responding
Bald wonders how this is possible since his men have killed all the train guards and captured the engineers
Hakuro advises the Blue Squad members to surrender peacefully
Bald silences Hakuro by shooting a hole in his left ear with his automail arm
Edward frees the engineers
Engineers take back control of the train and inform Edward that the car in front of Hakuro's suite is filled with water for the boiler
Edward communicates an ultimatum to the hijackers
Hijackers refuse to surrender
Edward uses the water from the tender car to flood the first-class car, subduing all the terrorists except Bald
Edward and Alphonse enter the first-class car simultaneously
Edward and Alphonse defeat Bald and disable his automail
Edward and Alphonse defeat Bald and disable his automail
The train arrives safely in East City
Bald is immediately taken into custody
The Colonel and his aide Lt. Hawkeye greet the Elrics at the station
Edward is annoyed by the Colonel and his aide
The Colonel and Edward trade insults
Bald escapes from custody using a hidden knife in his damaged automail and charges
The Colonel reveals his right glove is emblazoned with a unique Transmutation Circle
The Colonel snaps his fingers and subdues Bald with a fiery explosion
The military police restrain Bald again
The Colonel introduces himself as Roy Mustang the Flame Alchemist

Chapter 5: The Alchemist's Suffering.action: The 5th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Alchemist's Suffering"
Edward asks Colonel Roy Mustang to help him learn more about bio-alchemy
Mustang refers Edward to Shou Tucker, a State Alchemist specializing in chimeric alchemy
Mustang informs Edward that Tucker's chimera only said "I want to die" and did not live long
Edward is introduced to Tucker and explains his intentions
Tucker grants Edward access to his private home library of bio-alchemical texts
Edward delves into the books
Alphonse becomes acquainted with Tucker's daughter Nina and their dog Alexander
Alphonse drags Edward into their merriment
Edward and Alphonse are unable to find the information they need by sundown
Edward and Alphonse resolve to return the next day to continue their research
2nd Lt. Jean Havoc relays a message from the Colonel to Shou Tucker about his upcoming assessment
Nina asks her father to explain the assessment
Tucker explains that State Alchemists must submit yearly research results to qualify for grant money
Nina expresses confidence that her father will pass, which comforts Tucker
The Elric brothers return the next day to continue their studies
The Elric brothers decide to spend time playing with Nina
Nina reveals how lonely she has been in the past two years since her mother left and her father became engrossed in his research
Shou struggles desperately to draft a satisfactory assessment report
The boys return again the following day
The boys are surprised to find the house dark and apparently empty
The boys search for the Tuckers and reach a back room
Shou declares he has created a second talking chimera
Shou shows off the chimera to the astonished boys and teaches it to repeat Edward’s name
The chimera refers to Ed as "Big Brother" without prompt
Edward realizes the chimera was created by fusing Nina and Alexander together
Sewing-Life (the chimera) confirms this
Edward furiously condemns Tucker for playing with people’s lives
Tucker accuses Edward of being the same as him, performing Human Transmutation to test his abilities
Edward flies into a rage and begins beating Tucker savagely with his automail fist
Alphonse restrains Edward to keep Sewing-Life from being beaten to death
Alphonse forces Tucker into silence before he can say more
Alphonse apologizes to Nina, saying neither he nor Edward can undo the damage
The boys report the situation to East City HQ
Edward discusses the situation with Colonel Mustang
Edward acknowledges that, as a State Alchemist, he will likely see more evil and may be ordered to commit atrocities as a "human weapon"
Edward resolves to continue pressing forward on his path until he achieves his ultimate goal
Edward acknowledges that he is only human despite his abilities or position
Edward laments that he was unable to save even one little girl
a mysterious stranger with an x-shaped scar on his brow murders the guards at the gate of the Tucker estate
the stranger enters the house where a bruised and battered Shou is confiding to Nina that no one understands him
Shou confides to Nina that no one understands him
the stranger discovers Tucker and declares that, as an alchemist who has strayed from the path of God, Tucker must die
the stranger places his right hand on Tucker's face, displays a strange ability, and destroys Shou's body from the inside
Nina mourns for her deceased father in spite of what he did to her
the stranger notices Nina mourning, apologizes for what has happened to her, and repeats the process, asking her to go in peace
the stranger exits, asks God to care for the two souls he has just sent to the afterlife, and removes his sunglasses to reveal red eyes

Chapter 6: The Right Hand of Destruction.action: The 6th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Right Hand of Destruction"
Edward wakes from a nightmare about his mother
Edward and Alphonse head to military headquarters to inquire about the fates of Shou and Nina Tucker
Edward and Alphonse head to military headquarters to inquire about the fates of Shou and Nina Tucker
Lt. Hawkeye informs Edward and Alphonse that Shou was scheduled for decommissioning and court-martial, but both Shou and Nina have been murdered by an unknown assailant
At the crime scene, Lt. Colonel Maes Hughes and Major Alex Louis Armstrong confirm for Colonel Mustang that the manner of death matches the modus operandi of a man they have been investigating
Lust and Gluttony watch from the church bell tower as bloody riots tear up the streets in Reole
Lust and Gluttony ridicule the citizens' foolishness
Someone appearing to be Father Cornello joins Lust and Gluttony in their mockery
Lust, Gluttony, and the person appearing to be Cornello remark how the people of Reole have fallen prey to their scheme despite FullMetal's meddling
They remark that humans never learn, blindly resorting to hatred and bloodshed at the slightest provocation
The person appearing to be Cornello transforms into a new form at Lust's behest, revealing himself to be Envy
Gluttony disposes of Cray, who witnessed Envy's metamorphosis
Lust and Envy discuss the news of Shou Tucker's death and the danger of the elusive murderer around Roy Mustang or Edward Elric
Lust and Envy make plans to head to East City to investigate
Hughes and Armstrong explain to Mustang that the man they've been chasing is a serial killer targeting State Alchemists
Hughes and Armstrong describe the killer as having a large X-shaped scar on his forehead and dub him "Scar"
Hughes advises Mustang to lay low until Scar is caught
Mustang begins to worry for Fullmetal's safety
Mustang sends men to bring Edward to headquarters
Ed and Al sit out in the rain, remembering what their Teacher taught them and lamenting their failures
Ed and Al lament their failures to save their mother and Nina
A military policeman hails Ed and Al with orders to escort them to headquarters
Scar, hearing the name of one of his targets, approaches Ed and Al
Scar slays the officer with the strange power of his right hand
Scar turns towards the Elrics
Ed and Al attempt to flee through town
Ed and Al attempt to flee through town
Scar cuts off their escape path, forcing them to fight
Scar tears a hole in Alphonse's side, immobilizing him
Scar obliterates Edward's automail arm in an instant

Chapter 7: After the Rain.action: The 7th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "After the Rain"
Edward collapses to the ground after his automail arm is obliterated by Scar's attack
Alphonse, immobilized and terrified, cries out desperately for Edward's safety from the pavement
Edward, frozen in shock and despair, asks Scar if Alphonse is one of his assassination targets
Scar explains that Alphonse is not one of his priorities, but will kill anyone who gets in his way
Edward offers his own life in exchange for Scar's word that Alphonse will escape unharmed
Scar agrees to Edward's conditions and moves in for the kill
Alphonse begs Edward to flee, vehemently opposed to his brother's resignation
Scar is interrupted by the sudden appearance of Colonel Roy Mustang and his accompanying forces
Roy explains Scar's identity and his involvement in the deaths of Shou and Nina Tucker to Edward
Scar declares himself an agent of God's divine punishment
Roy attempts to take on Scar with his powers
Lt. Riza Hawkeye rescues Roy and reminds him that his Flame Alchemy does not work properly in the rain
Scar is distracted by Roy's actions and his own threats
Major Armstrong attacks Scar from behind and introduces himself as the Strong Arm Alchemist
Armstrong and Scar engage in battle
Armstrong observes and analyzes Scar's counterattacks
Armstrong deduces that Scar's power is alchemy with the reconstruction stage omitted, leaving matter in the deconstruction stage
Scar changes his attack pattern to exploit Armstrong's wide swings
Armstrong pulls back from combat, baiting Scar
Scar realizes the openings were intentional distractions for Mustang's forces to move into formation
Lt. Hawkeye opens fire on Scar with her rifle
Scar evades the shot, but the bullet grazes his face and knocks off his sunglasses, revealing his red irises
Armstrong and Mustang recognize Scar as an Ishvalan
Scar deconstructs the street beneath him and escapes into the sewer system
Edward rushes to Alphonse's side
Alphonse physically and verbally berates Edward for giving up on life and lectures him on the importance of surviving for the future and their goals
Mustang explains to the Elric brothers that the region of Ishval had been the center of a civil war that began thirteen years ago
An Amestrian soldier accidentally shoots and kills an Ishvalan child, starting the war
After seven years of fighting, the State Alchemists are sent into Ishval to eradicate all of the Ishvalans
Mustang asserts that there may be some justice in Scar's killing spree
Edward rebuts, saying Scar is disguising his own revenge as righteousness
Roy vows to hunt Scar down and eliminate him
Edward asserts that his lost arm makes it impossible to repair Al's armor while guaranteeing the integrity of his bound soul
Edward declares that his next move is to visit his automail mechanic

Chapter 8: The Road of Hope.action: The 8th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Road of Hope (希望の道, Kibō no Michi?)"
Edward sits next to Major Armstrong on the train, lamenting his new companion's presence
Edward's automail arm is destroyed and Alphonse is immobilized
It is decided Edward cannot travel to Resembool without an escort while Scar is on the loose
No one else is free or willing to accompany them
Major Armstrong agrees wholeheartedly to chaperon the Elric brothers on their journey
Lt. Colonel Hughes comes to see them off
The train begins to pull away
Edward explains to Major Armstrong that Resembool is a small eastern town whose growth was stunted by the Ishval Civil War
Edward explains the automail specialist in Resembool is a surgeon with weaponsmith experience and a close family friend
Lust sits a few rows away, keeping Fullmetal under surveillance, unbeknownst to Edward and Armstrong
The train makes a scheduled stop in a small town
Major Armstrong recognizes a passerby as Dr. Tim Marcoh, a former State Alchemist
Armstrong calls out to Marcoh
Marcoh panics and flees
Edward declares they will disembark and take a later train to Resembool
Armstrong, Edward, and Alphonse wander through the town asking for Dr. Marcoh
Armstrong, Edward, and Alphonse wander through the town asking for Dr. Marcoh
Armstrong, Edward, and Alphonse wander through the town asking for Dr. Marcoh
It becomes clear Marcoh is living under an assumed name, healing people with alchemy
They track Marcoh down
They track Marcoh down
They track Marcoh down
Marcoh meets them with hostility, fearing Armstrong was sent to arrest or assassinate him
Edward and Armstrong calm Marcoh down
Edward and Armstrong calm Marcoh down
Marcoh explains he ran from Ishval due to guilt over his research being used in the war
Marcoh says he is trying to make amends by healing others
Marcoh reveals his research was to create the Philosopher’s Stone, shocking the Elrics
Marcoh produces a vial filled with a gel-like red substance and explains it to be an experimental prototype stone
Edward asks to see Marcoh's research notes
Marcoh refuses and asks his guests to leave, asserting the information is evil
Armstrong inquires why Edward didn't simply steal Marcoh's stone
Edward replies that his conscience wouldn't allow him to take away the item that healed so many townspeople
Armstrong lets on that he does not plan to report Marcoh's whereabouts to his superiors
Marcoh appears at the station and hands a small note to Edward
Marcoh explains the note contains the location of his Philosopher's Stone research notes and expresses his belief in Edward's strength of will and talent
Marcoh returns to his house and finds Lust waiting for him in the dark
Marcoh gapes in horror
Lust reassures Marcoh that she has not come to take him back
Lust says the research has been continuing without Marcoh and his notes
Lust says the notes Marcoh stole may be dangerous if discovered by a State Alchemist like Fullmetal
Lust demands to know if Marcoh told Ed the location of the notes
Marcoh asserts that Edward will be able to figure out Lust's plans from the notes
Marcoh attacks Lust with alchemy
Lust shrugs off the attack and her wound seals itself
Lust takes a neighbor girl hostage and demands Marcoh tell her the location of his hidden data
Edward opens the note Marcoh gave him on the train to Resembool and reads "National Central Library, first branch"
Lust promises to wipe the village off the map if Marcoh tries anything strange
Lust makes plans to head to Central to destroy Marcoh's data before Fullmetal can reach it

Chapter 9: A Home with a Family Waiting.action: The 9th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "A Home with a Family Waiting"
The Elrics arrive in Resembool with Major Armstrong
The Elrics greet Winry Rockbell and her grandmother Pinako
Winry gives Ed a stern telling-off and harsh beating for destroying his automail arm
Pinako informs Ed that his automail leg needs to be readjusted to compensate for his growth
Pinako promises to have both limbs ready in three days
The Rockbell women begin working on Ed’s automail
Ed is given a spare prosthetic leg to walk on
Edward takes Alphonse’s advice and goes to visit his mother’s grave
Armstrong helps Pinako with household chores
Pinako asks Armstrong to tell her about Ed and Al’s life
Armstrong explains the Elrics’ notoriety and strength
Pinako recalls the night of the failed Human Transmutation and the events after
Pinako explains she has known the boys since birth and was a friend of their father
Pinako says their father disappeared long ago
Armstrong asks about Winry’s parents
Pinako responds that Winry’s parents went to the Ishval Civil War as doctors and were killed in action
Pinako reveals that Edward burned the Elric house to the ground after receiving his State Alchemist certification
Winry and Pinako connect Edward's new limbs to his nervous system and adjust them for use
Winry tries to explain to Edward that his new arm is less durable than its predecessor
Edward rushes off to repair Alphonse's armor
Edward collects all the broken pieces of Alphonse's armor
Edward transmutes the pieces back into their proper shape, avoiding the Blood Rune
Edward and Alphonse spar to make sure their repaired bodies function properly
Edward and Alphonse go inside for supper
Edward expresses optimism about not needing automail repairs in the future
Alphonse expresses gratitude to Winry and Pinako on behalf of Edward
The next morning, Edward, Alphonse, and Armstrong set out at dawn to catch the earliest train to Central City
Pinako asks them to come back for a visit if they want a home-cooked meal
Winry, groggy from three days and two nights of labor, gives them a cheerful wave goodbye from her bedroom
That evening, Winry wakes up and discovers a bolt she neglected to include in Edward's arm

Chapter 10: The Philosopher's Stone.action: The 10th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Philosopher's Stone"
Edward and Alphonse arrive in Central City
Major Armstrong introduces Edward and Alphonse to 2nd Lt. Maria Ross and Sergeant Denny Brosh
Ross and Brosh take over the Elrics' security detail from Major Armstrong
Ross and Brosh escort Edward and Alphonse to the National Central Library
Ross and Brosh explain that the First Branch building of the library had burned to the ground the day before
The First Branch building of the library burns to the ground in a suspected act of arson
Colonel Roy Mustang is scolded by Major General Hakuro for his unit's failure to apprehend Scar
Mustang brushes off the reprimand
Mustang confides in Lt. Riza Hawkeye and 2nd Lt. Jean Havoc about his anxiety to settle the matter with Scar and his secret ambitions
Lust joins Gluttony outside
Lust explains to Gluttony that she destroyed the library's first branch to prevent the Fullmetal Alchemist from finding Marcoh's notes
Gluttony's attention is drawn to the scent of an Ishvalan cloaked in blood
Gluttony asks Lust if he can eat the target
Lust orders Gluttony not to leave even a single hair behind
Scar becomes aware he is being followed in the sewers
Scar spies Gluttony behind him
Scar and Gluttony clash in the sewers
The sewer tunnel explodes and collapses
The Elrics and their escorts try to determine whether any of Marcoh's notes might have been stored in a different location
The Elrics and their escorts come up empty-handed
The Elrics and their escorts are referred to a young woman named Sheska, who had worked in the First Branch until her recent dismissal
The Elrics arrive at Sheska's house
The Elrics learn that Sheska was fired for reading the library's texts instead of doing her job
Sheska remembers Tim Marcoh's work being filed in the First Branch
Ed and Al thank Sheska and prepare to leave
Sheska informs them that she could reproduce the text perfectly for them with her photographic memory
Five days later, Sheska finishes the transcript
Edward thanks Sheska and takes the transcripts back to the library's main branch for study
Edward explains to Sgt. Brosh that many alchemists code their high-level alchemical documents
Edward says that if Sheska's copy is perfect and exact, the Elrics may be able to crack the code and decipher Marcoh's secret notes
The Elrics work for a week but make little headway into understanding Dr. Marcoh's research
Lt. Colonel Hughes visits the Elrics
Hughes mentions that the Tucker chimera case is still open
Hughes complains about the loss of important court-martial office records stored in the library's first branch
The Elrics inform Hughes that Sheska can reproduce the lost records from memory
Hughes gladly offers Sheska a new job in his office
The Elrics continue their research
The Elrics decipher Marcoh's code and discover the Philosopher's Stone's main ingredient is a live human being
The Elrics become furious upon learning the secret

Chapter 11: The Two Guardians.action: The 11th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Two Guardians"
Edward and Alphonse learn that the Philosopher's Stone is made of live humans and requires several human lives to fashion a single Stone
Edward and Alphonse become distraught
Edward and Alphonse ask Sgt. Brosh and 2nd Lt. Ross to disavow knowledge of their discovery
military police find Scar's jacket near the wreckage of the sewer tunnel
Colonel Mustang and his men are summoned to the scene to investigate
Lt. Hawkeye determines that the bloodstains on the jacket suggest the serial killer was badly injured
Gluttony laments nearby that his prey managed to escape him
Lust consoles Gluttony
Lust makes plans to return to Central City and inform their Father of the new development
Ed and Al mope in their hotel room, cursing their luck and wondering if their original forms are forever lost to them
Edward mentions to Alphonse that there is something important he always wanted to discuss but was too afraid to bring up
Edward is interrupted when Major Armstrong bursts into the room
Major Armstrong pressures Brosh and Ross into telling him why the boys have been glum
Armstrong weeps over the boys' misfortune and laments how cruel the truth can be
Edward is reminded of Dr. Marcoh's cryptic final message about a "truth behind the truth"
Edward resumes his investigation with the help of the others
Edward looks at a city map denoting all four Alchemical Laboratories in Central City
Edward notes a supposedly abandoned Fifth Laboratory within the city limits
Edward proposes that the Fifth Laboratory is likely where Philosopher's Stones were manufactured, citing its adjacency to Central Prison
Alphonse asks Armstrong who was in charge of Lab 5 before it was shut down
Armstrong informs them that Lab 5 was under the jurisdiction of State Alchemist and Brigadier General Basque Grand, who was recently assassinated by Scar
Armstrong mentions the situation could become complicated if a Brigadier General or higher rank were involved
Armstrong prepares to investigate the matter quietly on his own
Armstrong advises Brosh and Ross not to speak of this to anyone
Armstrong demands that the Elric brothers remain in their hotel and keep away from Lab 5 until further notice
The boys sneak away at night to investigate the facility
The boys discover a soldier guarding the supposedly abandoned building
Edward realizes the only covert way in is through the narrow vent system
Edward opts to enter the lab through the vents
Alphonse remains outside the lab
Two armor-clad figures, 48 and 66, sense the intruders and stir
66 makes plans to go after Alphonse
48 prepares to take on Edward
Edward notices the facility still has electricity and confirms the lab is in use
66 descends upon Alphonse and attacks him with a meat cleaver
66 remarks that his true identity is a shocking secret
Edward reaches a large hall inside the lab and notices a huge, bloodstained Five-Point Transmutation Circle on the floor
Edward asks himself aloud if this is where the Philosopher's Stone was created
48 answers in the affirmative, emerging from the shadows to inform Edward that his presence cannot go unpunished, especially since he appears to know about the Philosopher's Stone
Edward transmutes the outer plating of his automail arm into a blade
Edward engages 48 in battle
Edward discovers that 48 is an empty suit of armor just like Alphonse
48 explains that he was a serial killer known as "Slicer" who had been placed on death row and used as a test subject in Soul Binding experiments
48 shows Edward that his Blood Rune is hidden inside his helmet
48 informs Edward that he must destroy the rune to win before preparing for a second assault

Chapter 12: The Definition of Human.action: The 12th chapter of the Fullmetal Alchemist manga is titled "The Definition of Human"
Ross and Brosh are horrified to discover that the Elric brothers have snuck out of their hotel room in the middle of the night
Ross and Brosh rush angrily to their charges' most likely location—abandoned Laboratory 5
Edward continues his fight with the armored guard known as Number 48
Edward notices an unpleasant twinge suggesting something has gone wrong with his automail arm
Edward remembers Winry told him his new model was less durable than its predecessor
Edward resolves to finish the bout quickly
Edward finds himself on the run from the Slicer's increasingly vicious assault
Edward earns a collection of small wounds as he barely maneuvers out of the razor-sharp sword's path

Chapter 107 is released in the U.S.A. on December 20, 2011
The Last Battle is identified as the 107th chapter of Fullmetal Alchemist

Chapter 108: Journey's End.action: Journey's End is identified as the 108th and final chapter of Fullmetal Alchemist
Ed spars with Father, who is rapidly losing control over his power
Father loses control over his power
Father impales Greed and drains his Philosopher's Stone
Greed turns part of Father into charcoal before being killed
Greed fades away, content to have made friends
Ed smashes a hole in Father
Father releases Truth and all of the souls of Xerxes in his body
Father reverts to his tiny homunculus form before the Gate of Truth
God tells Father it is his fate to fall back into the Gate into eternal despair
Father falls back into the Gate of Truth
The battered victors are in shock over Al's sacrifice
Ling offers his Philosopher's Stone to get Al back
Ed reminds Ling they swore not to use a Philosopher's Stone to recover Al's body
Van Hohenheim offers himself as the toll to save his son
Ed rejects Hohenheim's offer, but acknowledges him as his father
Ed thanks everyone who is left
Ed draws a human transmutation circle
Ed goes before Truth and the Gate
Truth asks Ed what the cost will be now
Ed replies he will pay with the Gate, the source of Alchemy
Truth asks how Ed will manage without his greatest skill
Ed replies "Even without Alchemy, I still have my friends."
Truth says this is the correct answer and concedes defeat
Ed transmutes his Gate
Ed finds Al, body and soul in one again
The two brothers return to everyone's amazement
Hohenheim welcomes his son back
Al notices the amazing sensations that have now returned to his real body
Hohenheim walks off into the dust
Olivier and Alex resolve to rescue any civilians trapped in the rubble their battle has created
Mustang's radio team broadcast their version of the story, revealing how the upper echelon plotted on alchemically harming the populace, though not revealing Bradley's identity
Riza is visited in the hospital by Rebecca, who comments on the hard task of reconciling the Briggs attack on Central with the main story
Mustang is met by Dr. Knox and Marcoh

Marcoh restores Mustang's sight with his Philosopher's Stone in exchange for the promise that Roy will ensure a full restoration of Ishvalan rights and the opportunity for Marcoh to be a physician among them
Ling and Lan Fan discuss Ling’s future as emperor of Xing
Lan Fan requests that the other families not suffer as a result of Ling’s success
Ling easily accepts Lan Fan’s proposal
May hears the conversation and bursts into tears of relief
The Xingese depart for their home, bearing Fu’s body
Pinako approaches Trisha's grave
Pinako discovers Hohenheim, who has died in peace at his wife's gravesite
Scar recuperates in a makeshift hospital
Olivier and Miles visit Scar with a proposal to aid in the restoration of Ishvalan society
Olivier and Miles depart to begin their work
Olivier asks Scar for his real name one last time
Scar replies that he has no need for a name and can be called whatever one likes
Ed and Al walk home after Father's defeat
Al is not recognized at first by Winry's dog, Den, due to his new appearance
Winry walks out and, after a moment of shock at Al's appearance, runs out to embrace Ed and Al
Pinako listens to Central's radio, which reports on Brigadier General Mustang's efforts to restore the Ishvalans and his pact with Emperor Ling
Ed fixes Winry's roof, reflecting on no longer having Alchemy but feeling content
Two years later, Führer Grumman talks to Miss Bradley
Selim, now more childlike, runs in, free to be a child again
Al decides to leave for Xing to learn alkahestry
Al pays his last respects to the Hughes family as he leaves
Al takes Zampano and Jerso with him on his journey
Ed and Winry wait for Ed's train to head west
At the last second, Ed and Winry confess their feelings for each other
Edward uses Equivalent Exchange to propose to Winry
The final page displays a collage of pictures of the characters
Havoc is shown struggling in rehab while recovering
The side by side graves of Trisha and Hohenheim are shown
Roy, who appears to have regained his sight and been promoted, is pictured with Riza
Scar, now long-haired, is shown alongside Miles
Emperor Ling Yao is pictured with Lan Fan
A family picture of the Elrics, including Ed, Winry, their two children, as well as Al and May Chang, is shown
